speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: There’s not a man I meet but doth salute me

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: As if I were their well-acquainted friend,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And everyone doth call me by my name.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Some tender money to me; some invite me;

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Some other give me thanks for kindnesses;

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Some offer me commodities to buy.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Even now a tailor called me in his shop

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And showed me silks that he had bought for me,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And therewithal took measure of my body.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Sure these are but imaginary wiles,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And Lapland sorcerers inhabit here.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Master, here’s the gold you sent

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: me for. What, have you got the picture of old Adam

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: new-appareled?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What gold is this? What Adam dost thou mean?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Not that Adam that kept the

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Paradise, but that Adam that keeps the prison; he

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: that goes in the calf’s skin that was killed for the

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Prodigal; he that came behind you, sir, like an evil

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: angel, and bid you forsake your liberty.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I understand thee not.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: No? Why, ’tis a plain case: he

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: that went like a bass viol in a case of leather; the

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: man, sir, that, when gentlemen are tired, gives

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: them a sob and ’rests them; he, sir, that takes pity

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: on decayed men and gives them suits of durance; he

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: that sets up his rest to do more exploits with his

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: mace than a morris-pike.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What, thou mean’st an

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: officer?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Ay, sir, the sergeant of the band;

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: he that brings any man to answer it that breaks his

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: band; one that thinks a man always going to bed

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: and says “God give you good rest.”

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Well, sir, there rest in your

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: foolery. Is there any ships puts forth tonight? May

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: we be gone?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Why, sir, I brought you word an

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: hour since that the bark “Expedition” put forth tonight,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: and then were you hindered by the sergeant

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: to tarry for the hoy “Delay”. Here are the angels that

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: you sent for to deliver you.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: The fellow is distract, and so am I,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And here we wander in illusions.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Some blessèd power deliver us from hence!

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Well met, well met, Master Antipholus.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: I see, sir, you have found the goldsmith now.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Is that the chain you promised me today?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Satan, avoid! I charge thee, tempt me not.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Master, is this Mistress Satan?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: It is the devil.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Nay, she is worse; she is the

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: devil’s dam, and here she comes in the habit of a

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: light wench. And thereof comes that the wenches

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: say “God damn me”; that’s as much to say “God

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: make me a light wench.” It is written they appear

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: to men like angels of light. Light is an effect of fire,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: and fire will burn: ergo, light wenches will burn.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Come not near her.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Your man and you are marvelous merry, sir.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Will you go with me? We’ll mend our dinner here.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Master, if you do, expect spoon

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: meat, or bespeak a long spoon.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Why, Dromio?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Marry, he must have a long

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: spoon that must eat with the devil.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Avoid then, fiend! What tell’st thou me of supping?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thou art, as you are all, a sorceress.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I conjure thee to leave me and be gone.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Give me the ring of mine you had at dinner

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Or, for my diamond, the chain you promised,

speaker: COURTESAN
text: And I’ll be gone, sir, and not trouble you.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Some devils ask but the parings

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: of one’s nail, a rush, a hair, a drop of blood, a pin, a

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: nut, a cherrystone; but she, more covetous, would

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: have a chain. Master, be wise. An if you give it her,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: the devil will shake her chain and fright us with it.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: I pray you, sir, my ring or else the chain.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: I hope you do not mean to cheat me so.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Avaunt, thou witch!—Come, Dromio, let us go.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: “Fly pride,” says the peacock.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Mistress, that you know.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Now, out of doubt Antipholus is mad;

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Else would he never so demean himself.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: A ring he hath of mine worth forty ducats,

speaker: COURTESAN
text: And for the same he promised me a chain.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Both one and other he denies me now.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: The reason that I gather he is mad,

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Besides this present instance of his rage,

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Is a mad tale he told today at dinner

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Of his own doors being shut against his entrance.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Belike his wife, acquainted with his fits,

speaker: COURTESAN
text: On purpose shut the doors against his way.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: My way is now to hie home to his house

speaker: COURTESAN
text: And tell his wife that, being lunatic,

speaker: COURTESAN
text: He rushed into my house and took perforce

speaker: COURTESAN
text: My ring away. This course I fittest choose,

speaker: COURTESAN
text: For forty ducats is too much to lose.
